title: 10 best herbal teas for hangover

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's vasodilatory properties may help improve blood flow and oxygenation to the brain and other organs, potentially alleviating hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
Liver-protective effects: Some studies suggest that ginkgo biloba extracts may have liver-protective effects, which could help mitigate liver damage caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, teas helps with hangover because of its unique combination of flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide compounds. These compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate hangover symptoms. Ginkgo biloba tea may aid in improving blood flow to the brain, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting the removal of toxins from the body, thereby alleviating symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea associated with hangovers. Its potential to reduce hangover severity makes it a popular remedy.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidant compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
Terpenoids (specifically, bilobalide and ginkgolides): These terpenoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms like nausea and vomiting.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce hangover symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Seeds: They contain ginkgotoxine, which is thought to have a sedative effect and may help reduce hangover symptoms such as nausea and headaches.

Leaves: (again, as they are the most commonly used part) Some studies suggest that the flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba leaves may help alleviate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 ounce of ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of boiling water for steeping.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for each 8 ounce cup.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release flavors and oils.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove loose leaves.
Allow the tea to cool for 5 minutes before serving and consuming immediately.

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Chamomile tea contains antioxidants that help neutralize the oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms like headaches and fatigue.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which may help reduce inflammation in the body caused by binge drinking, alleviating symptoms like nausea, stomach upset, and joint pain.

GABA-boosting flavonoids: Chamomile tea contains flavonoids that can increase the production of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep and relaxation. This can help alleviate hangover symptoms like anxiety, jitteriness, and insomnia.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, teas helps with hangover because of its potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The tea's active compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, work to calm the digestive system and alleviate nausea, headaches, and dizziness often associated with hangovers. Additionally, chamomile's natural relaxant properties can help reduce stress and anxiety caused by excessive drinking, promoting a faster recovery and alleviating the symptoms of a hangover.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that acts as a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, helping to alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue.
Luteolin: A flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce hangover-related inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
α-Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and calming effects, which may help to soothe hangover symptoms such as stomach upset and irritability.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make teas for hangover due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help alleviate symptoms such as headaches and nausea.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids that may aid in reducing hangover symptoms, such as dizziness and stomach upset.
Seeds: They contain apigenin, an antioxidant that may help alleviate hangover symptoms, such as anxiety and insomnia, due to its calming effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried matricaria chamomilla flowers by collecting them in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Grind 1 teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of ground matricaria chamomilla flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea and serve immediately or let it cool down to enjoy at room temperature.

remedy_properties: Digestive soothing: Peppermint oil's natural anti-inflammatory compounds can help calm nausea and soothe digestive discomfort caused by excessive drinking.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in peppermint tea may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to hangover symptoms like headaches and fatigue.
Stomach acid reduction: Peppermint oil's menthol content has been shown to relax the stomach muscles, reducing the production of stomach acid that can exacerbate nausea, heartburn, and bloating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with hangover because it aids in digestion and reduces nausea. The menthol content in peppermint helps to calm the stomach and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and irritable bowel. Additionally, peppermint's natural cooling effect helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the body's discomfort. By promoting hydration and alleviating digestive discomfort, peppermint teas can provide relief and comfort to those experiencing hangover symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for hangover recovery.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps with hangover by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
Menthone: A terpene found in peppermint, menthone has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate headaches and nausea associated with hangovers.

Limonene: A monoterpene found in peppermint, limonene has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help mitigate hangover symptoms such as fatigue and stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The peppermint leaves are used to make teas for hangover due to their calming and soothing effects on the digestive system, helping to alleviate nausea and stomach discomfort associated with hangovers.
Stems: The stems of the peppermint plant contain menthol, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent that can help to reduce headaches and alleviate the pain associated with hangovers.
Leaves: The menthol and menthone present in peppermint leaves can also help to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation in the body, further aiding in hangover relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 grams of fresh leaves from the plant in the early morning when dew is present.
Chop the harvested leaves finely using a sharp knife or a dedicated herb cutter.
Steep 1 teaspoon of chopped leaves in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before serving and consuming immediately.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant-rich flavonoids: Maypop tea's flavonoid content helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which contributes to hangover symptoms like headaches and fatigue.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory properties of Maypop tea, including its caffeic acid and quercetin content, can help alleviate nausea, vomiting, and stomach discomfort often experienced during hangovers.
Digestive soothers: Maypop tea's natural relaxants, such as passionflower flavonoids, may help calm digestive issues like diarrhea, bloating, and cramps commonly associated with hangovers.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, teas helps with hangover because of its natural properties that alleviate symptoms associated with overindulgence. The tea's flavonoids and alkaloids, such as passiflorine and harmine, have been shown to have sedative and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce hangover symptoms like headaches and nausea. Additionally, the tea's ability to regulate the body's internal clock and promote relaxation may also contribute to its hangover-relieving properties, providing a soothing and restorative effect on the body.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which often exacerbate hangover discomfort.
Harmane: A tryptamine alkaloid found in Passiflora incarnata, Harmane has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate hangover pain and discomfort.

Isovitexin: A flavonoid glycoside present in Passiflora incarnata, Isovitexin has been demonstrated to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used to make teas due to their high content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which help in reducing inflammation and anxiety associated with hangovers.

Fruits: Fruits are used to make teas because they contain high amounts of vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C and potassium, which can help replenish fluids and electrolytes lost during intoxication.
Roots: Roots are used to make teas due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom and dry them thoroughly for later use.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the liquid through a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool and then refrigerate it for later consumption or drink it immediately.

remedy_properties: Sedative and relaxing compounds: Valerenic acid, a primary component of valerian root, has sedative and relaxing effects that can help calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress associated with hangovers.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian contains anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the liver and other tissues caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms like nausea and fatigue.
Digestive soothing effects: The volatile oils present in valerian root have been shown to soothe digestive issues such as nausea, bloating, and stomach cramps, which are common hangover symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, teas helps with hangover because it contains valerenic acid, which acts as a natural relaxant and anti-inflammatory agent. It helps to calm the mind and body, reducing symptoms of nausea, headaches, and fatigue associated with hangovers. Additionally, valerian tea has been shown to improve sleep quality and reduce anxiety, making it an effective remedy for those suffering from the aftermath of excessive alcohol consumption. Its soothing effects can help alleviate hangover discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This sesquiterpene acts as a GABA_A receptor antagonist, which can help regulate the body's response to stress and alleviate hangover symptoms such as anxiety and nausea.
Valeranone: This valerenane sesquiterpene has sedative properties, helping to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making it easier to recover from hangover.

Acidic phenolic compounds: These compounds, such as valerenic acid and valeranone, have antioxidant properties that can help neutralize oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, contributing to hangover relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are the most commonly used part for making teas due to their high concentration of valerenic acids, which are responsible for the plant's sedative properties.
Roots: The roots also contain valeranone and valerenal, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Roots: Roots of Valeriana officinalis have been traditionally used as a natural remedy to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which can be a major contributor to hangover symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and rhizomes from a trusted source in the morning.
Dry the harvested roots and rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Chop the dried roots and rhizomes into small pieces weighing 1 gram per 8 ounce serving.
Steep the chopped roots and rhizomes in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea and let it cool before serving in a 8 ounce cup for optimal flavor and benefits.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the severity of hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea by soothing the digestive tract.
Licoricidin: This component has been shown to have analgesic and anti-nausea effects, making it effective in reducing hangover-related pain and discomfort.

Isoliquiritigenin: This flavonoid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body after excessive alcohol consumption, potentially alleviating hangover symptoms like fatigue and brain fog.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with hangover because of its anti-inflammatory properties and the presence of glycyrrhizin, a compound that can help alleviate headache and stomach discomfort. The tea's soothing effects can also aid in reducing nausea and promoting digestion, making it a popular remedy for those suffering from hangovers. Additionally, licorice root has been traditionally used to calm the stomach and alleviate irritability, providing relief from hangover symptoms and supporting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra teas help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin helps with hangover by inhibiting the action of the enzyme 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, which is involved in the production of cortisol, a hormone that can exacerbate hangover symptoms.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to mitigate the negative effects of alcohol consumption on the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate hangover symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to soothe the stomach and reduce hangover symptoms.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to alleviate hangover symptoms, such as nausea and headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5 to 10 dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in late summer or early autumn.
Chop the roots into small pieces and steep 1 teaspoon of the chopped roots in 8 ounces of boiling water.
Reduce heat to low and let the tea simmer for 5 to 10 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids and discard the solids.
Store the tea in airtight containers and consume it within 24 hours to preserve its medicinal properties.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ginseng helps to regulate stress hormones like cortisol, which can become imbalanced after heavy drinking and exacerbate hangover symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: Ginseng's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals produced by alcohol consumption, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to hangover symptoms.
Glycyrrhizin content: The glycyrrhizin in ginseng has anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to alleviate headache, nausea, and other discomforts associated with hangovers.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng, also known as ginseng, teas helps with hangover because it contains compounds that stimulate the body's natural detoxification processes. Ginsenosides, the active ingredients in ginseng, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate headaches and fatigue associated with hangovers. Additionally, ginseng teas may aid in replenishing essential nutrients and balancing blood sugar levels, which can help mitigate the negative effects of excessive alcohol consumption. This can lead to a faster recovery and reduced hangover symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation, improving blood circulation, and exerting antioxidant effects to mitigate oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

Ginsenol: A phenolic compound derived from ginsenosides, ginsenol has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential hangover-relieving effects.

Ginsenoside Rg1: A specific ginsenoside with anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties, ginsenoside Rg1 may help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation, improving cognitive function, and providing antioxidant protection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The primary part used in ginseng teas for hangovers, as it contains the most potent bioactive compounds, including ginsenosides.
Leaves: Used in combination with roots to create a more balanced tea, as they are said to enhance the effects of ginsenosides.
Roots: Also used as a secondary part, as some believe it can help to detoxify and replenish energy after a hangover.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 whole panax ginseng roots with a healthy appearance and store them in a cool dry place.
Clean the roots under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris and pat them dry with paper towels.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine 2 teaspoons of the chopped roots with 1 quart of boiling water in a pot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 30-40 minutes to release the active compounds and then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth.
Discard the solids and store the tea in a clean glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.

remedy_properties: Laxative properties: Dandelion tea helps to stimulate bowel movements, which can relieve hangover-related digestive issues such as constipation, bloating, and nausea.

Diuretic properties: Dandelion tea increases urine production, which can help to flush out excess toxins and alcohol from the body, reducing dehydration and headache symptoms associated with hangovers.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tea contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hangover-related fatigue, headaches, and muscle aches.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, teas helps with hangover because of its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's high levels of potassium, vitamins, and minerals help replenish lost nutrients and electrolytes, alleviating dehydration and stomach discomfort. The antioxidants present in dandelion tea also aid in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption. Additionally, dandelion's bitter compounds may help stimulate digestion and relieve nausea, making it a popular natural remedy for hangover relief.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These triterpenoid compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce hangover-related inflammation and oxidative stress.
Flavonoids: Specifically, quercetin and kaempferol, are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help mitigate hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates may help stabilize blood sugar levels and promote gut health, which can aid in alleviating hangover symptoms such as nausea and stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a compound called taraxasterol, which is believed to help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive system.
Roots: They contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including saponins, which may help mitigate hangover symptoms and support liver health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers from a clean and pesticide-free area weighing about 30 grams.
Rinse the harvested taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers thoroughly under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Chop the cleaned taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers into small pieces using a sharp knife weighing about 10 grams.
Steep 10 grams of chopped taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in a liter of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the taraxacum officinale tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Lemon balm tea's antioxidants help neutralize the free radicals caused by excessive alcohol consumption, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

Calming effects: Lemon balm's calming compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, can soothe an upset stomach and reduce nausea and headaches associated with hangovers, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.

Digestive soothing properties: Lemon balm tea has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate digestive discomfort, bloating, and cramps caused by alcohol consumption, allowing the body to recover more quickly.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, teas helps with hangover because of its unique combination of properties. The tea's calming effects can help soothe an upset stomach, reducing nausea and discomfort. Additionally, Melissa officinalis contains compounds that may aid in reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate headaches and other hangover symptoms. Its natural and gentle approach can provide relief and support the body in recovering from a night of excess.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that acts as a potent antioxidant, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hangover symptoms.
Lemon balm flavonoids (including rutin and hesperidin): Flavonoids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
Terpinen-4-ol: A terpene with sedative and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce anxiety, insomnia, and other hangover-related symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their calming and digestive properties to help alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea and headaches.
Stems: May be used to provide a soothing and calming effect, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
Buds: Similar to leaves, buds are used for their calming properties and may help alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in early morning for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested melissa leaves and flowers using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried melissa leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried melissa leaves in boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the melissa tea and discard the solids to enjoy a calming and soothing cup.

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: St. John's Wort contains a variety of antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The plant's bioactive compounds, such as hyperforin and adhyperforin, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate hangover symptoms like headaches, fatigue, and nausea.

Adaptogenic effects: St. John's Wort has been traditionally used to promote mental clarity, reduce stress, and improve mood, which can be beneficial in alleviating the psychological symptoms of a hangover, such as irritability, anxiety, and depression.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, teas helps with hangover because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids and hyperforin present in the plant have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to hangover symptoms. Additionally, St John's Wort has been found to have a stimulating effect on the body, helping to alleviate fatigue and promote a sense of well-being, making it a popular remedy for those seeking relief from hangover symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This prenylated phenolic compound has potent antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hangover symptoms.

Hypaconitine (also known as Hyperforin's isomer, pseudohypericin): This alkaloid has shown significant anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate hangover-related pain and discomfort.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): This type of polyphenolic compound has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-nausea effects, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, the leaves are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce hangover symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers are a key ingredient due to their high content of flavonoids and other compounds that may help alleviate hangover symptoms, such as nausea and headaches.
Buds: The buds are used for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce hangover-related discomfort and promote recovery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in the late morning.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a warm place for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Chop 2-3 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves into small pieces.
Steep the chopped flowers and leaves in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark teas helps with hangover because it contains compounds that alleviate symptoms of dehydration, nausea, and fatigue. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce hangover headaches and body aches, while its antimicrobial properties can aid in the digestion of heavy meals and prevent stomach upset. Additionally, Jesuit's bark has been traditionally used as a natural diuretic, helping to flush out excess toxins and fluids from the body after excessive drinking.
remedy_properties: Quinine: This active compound helps to reduce blood ethanol levels and alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers.

Tannins: Tannins in Jesuit's Bark Tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing discomfort and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in the tea, such as limonene and terpinen-4-ol, may help to alleviate headaches and dizziness associated with hangovers by acting as natural pain relievers and sedatives.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help to reduce hangover symptoms by calming the digestive system.
Barks: Containing a compound called cinchonine, the barks have natural analgesic and anti-nausea properties that aid in alleviating hangover discomfort.
Rhizomes: With a high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, rhizomes help to reduce inflammation and promote overall well-being during hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Rinse dried cinchona pubescens bark with cold water to remove impurities and debris.
Measure one teaspoon of dried cinchona pubescens bark for every cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture in a tea infuser or strainer for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving hot with honey and lemon if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild mint teas helps with hangover because its natural antispasmodic properties help alleviate digestive discomfort, such as nausea and cramping, that often accompanies a night of heavy drinking. The tea's calming effects also soothe irritability and headaches, while its anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and relieve pain in the body. Additionally, wild mint's ability to stimulate digestion helps break down remaining toxins from last night's indulgence, promoting overall bodily recovery.
remedy_properties: Peppermint's digestive soothing compounds: Wild mint tea contains menthol and menthone, which can help ease nausea and stomach upset by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract.
Mint's anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in wild mint tea have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce hangover-related headaches and inflammation.

Mint's calming effects on the nervous system: The volatile oils in wild mint tea, particularly menthol and linalool, can help calm the stomach and alleviate anxiety and stress associated with hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild mint plant contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing digestive issues and alleviating hangover symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the wild mint plant have been traditionally used to treat headaches and nausea, common hangover symptoms that can be effectively managed with its medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha arvensis leaves by pinching off small bunches from healthy plants during morning dew.
Rinse harvested leaves gently with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried or 2 teaspoons of fresh mentha arvensis leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard solids before drinking hot tea immediately.
Enjoy warm tea within 30-60 minutes after consumption to potentially alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose teas helps with hangover because its soothing and calming properties help to alleviate symptoms of dehydration, such as headaches and fatigue. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce swelling in the body, which can occur due to excess sodium consumption during drinking. Additionally, beach rose's natural antacids help neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from nausea and digestive discomfort. As a result, this herbal tea offers a gentle and effective way to combat hangover symptoms without harsh chemicals or artificial additives.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Beach rose tea is rich in antioxidants, which help to neutralize free radicals in the body caused by excessive alcohol consumption, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hangovers.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue by reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Glycogen replenishment: Beach rose tea has been shown to help restore glycogen levels in the body, which can become depleted due to excessive drinking, helping to alleviate hangover symptoms like dizziness, weakness, and lightheadedness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the beach rose plant contain bioactive compounds that help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting digestion.
Barks: The barks of the beach rose plant are rich in tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe hangover headaches and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa petals and measure out one teaspoon of dried flowers or two teaspoons of fresh flowers.
Heat filtered water in a saucepan to a temperature of 195°f to 205°f for steeping.
Combine the measured rosa rugosa flowers with the hot water and allow it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to drinking temperature and enjoy within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea teas helps with hangover because of its natural ingredients that can alleviate symptoms such as dehydration, nausea, and fatigue. The antioxidants and flavonoids in herbal tea, particularly peppermint, ginger, and chamomile, work to soothe the stomach and calm the mind. Additionally, herbal teas like licorice root and lemon balm have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce hangover-induced pain and discomfort. By promoting hydration and relaxation, herbal tea can provide a gentle and effective way to recover from a night of overindulgence.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal teas like peppermint, chamomile, and ginger contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the body and alleviate symptoms like headaches and nausea associated with hangovers.

Antioxidants: Many herbal teas, such as green tea and hibiscus tea, are rich in antioxidants that can help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to hangover symptoms like fatigue and irritability.

Digestive soothing compounds: Herbal teas like ginger, peppermint, and licorice root contain compounds that can help soothe the digestive system and alleviate nausea, stomach cramps, and diarrhea often experienced during a hangover.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help alleviate headaches and nausea associated with hangovers.
Barks: The barks contain tannins that can help reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried green tea leaves or five fresh leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the green tea in boiling water for exactly three minutes and then strain it gently.
Combine one tablespoon of honey with a squeeze of fresh lime juice and add it to the brewed green tea.
Allow the mixture to cool down to room temperature before refrigerating it for at least thirty minutes.
Consume the cooled herbal tea slowly over the course of an hour to help alleviate hangover symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato teas helps with hangover because of its unique blend of herbs that work in harmony to alleviate symptoms. supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hangover that are treated with herbal teas are various digestive issues, such as indigestion, bloating, and nausea. Ginger tea is commonly used to alleviate these symptoms, while peppermint tea can help soothe an upset stomach. Dandelion root tea may also be consumed to aid digestion and relieve mild headaches. Additionally, some herbs like passionflower and lemon balm are known for their calming properties, which can help with anxiety and stress, similar to the relief sought from a hangover remedy.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hangover are natural remedies made from various herbs and plants that aid in alleviating hangover symptoms. They work by reducing inflammation, replenishing lost fluids, and soothing digestive issues. Examples include peppermint tea for nausea, ginger tea for stomach relief, and chamomile tea for calming anxiety. By consuming these teas, individuals can improve their overall well-being, reduce hangover severity, and enhance their quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with hangover is a blend of ginger, peppermint, and dandelion root. Ginger tea helps alleviate nausea and stomach discomfort, while peppermint tea soothes the digestive system and calms the mind. Dandelion root tea supports liver function and aids in detoxification. This trio works together to alleviate hangover symptoms, reducing inflammation, and replenishing essential nutrients. Drinking these teas the morning after can help alleviate hangover symptoms and get you back on track.
